questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the matrix $A=\begin{bmatrix}4 & 1\\ 2 & 3\end{bmatrix}$, the sum of its eigenvalues is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The sum of the eigenvalues of a square matrix equals its trace (sum of the leading-diagonal entries).

**Working.**
$$\lambda_1+\lambda_2=\operatorname{tr}(A)=4+3=7$$

**Answer.** $7$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the matrix $A=\begin{bmatrix}4 & 1\\ 2 & 3\end{bmatrix}$, the product of its eigenvalues is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The product of the eigenvalues of a square matrix equals its determinant.

**Working.**
$$\lambda_1\lambda_2=\det(A)=(4)(3)-(1)(2)=10$$

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The rank of $\begin{bmatrix}1 & 2 & 3\\ 2 & 4 & 6\\ 1 & 1 & 1\end{bmatrix}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rank = number of linearly independent rows.

**Working.** Row $R_2=2R_1$, so it is dependent and contributes nothing. Rows $R_1=(1,2,3)$ and $R_3=(1,1,1)$ are not proportional, hence independent.
$$\text{rank}=2$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The largest eigenvalue of $A=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 1\\ 1 & 2\end{bmatrix}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Solve the characteristic equation $\det(A-\lambda I)=0$.

**Working.**
$$(2-\lambda)^2-1=0\;\Rightarrow\;2-\lambda=\pm 1\;\Rightarrow\;\lambda=1,\,3$$

**Answer.** Largest eigenvalue $=3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $A=\begin{bmatrix}1 & 2\\ 3 & 4\end{bmatrix}$, the matrix $A^2-5A$ equals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cayley–Hamilton: a matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation $\lambda^2-(\operatorname{tr}A)\lambda+\det A=0$.

**Working.** Here $\operatorname{tr}A=5$ and $\det A=4-6=-2$, so
$$A^2-5A-2I=0\;\Rightarrow\;A^2-5A=2I$$

**Answer.** $2I$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The system $x+y=2,\; x+ky=4$ fails to have a unique solution when $k=$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A $2\times2$ system has a unique solution iff the coefficient determinant is non-zero.

**Working.**
$$\det\begin{bmatrix}1 & 1\\ 1 & k\end{bmatrix}=k-1=0\;\Rightarrow\;k=1$$
At $k=1$ the lines are parallel (here inconsistent), so no unique solution.

**Answer.** $k=1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $A$ is symmetric and $B$ is skew-symmetric of the same order, then $A+B$ is in general
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $A^{T}=A$ and $B^{T}=-B$. Test $(A+B)^{T}$.

**Working.**
$$(A+B)^{T}=A^{T}+B^{T}=A-B\neq \pm(A+B)$$
in general. In fact every square matrix splits uniquely into a symmetric and a skew-symmetric part, so the sum is generally neither.

**Answer.** Neither symmetric nor skew-symmetric.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The determinant of the upper-triangular matrix $A=\begin{bmatrix}2 & 5 & 7\\ 0 & 3 & 1\\ 0 & 0 & 5\end{bmatrix}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a triangular matrix the eigenvalues are the diagonal entries, and the determinant is their product.

**Working.**
$$\det(A)=2\times 3\times 5=30$$

**Answer.** $30$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following are ALWAYS true for a real symmetric matrix?
options: Eigenvectors of distinct eigenvalues are orthogonal
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Spectral theorem for real symmetric matrices.

**Working.**
- Real eigenvalues — TRUE.
- Eigenvectors for distinct eigenvalues are orthogonal — TRUE.
- Always invertible — FALSE (a zero eigenvalue is allowed).
- Orthogonally diagonalizable, $A=Q\Lambda Q^{T}$ — TRUE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (d).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A $3\times3$ matrix has eigenvalues $1,\,2,\,4$. Its determinant is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\det(A)=\prod \lambda_i$.

**Working.**
$$\det(A)=1\times 2\times 4=8$$

**Answer.** $8$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $\lambda=2$ is an eigenvalue of $A$, then the corresponding eigenvalue of $A^{3}-2A$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** If $Av=\lambda v$, then $p(A)v=p(\lambda)v$ for any polynomial $p$.

**Working.**
$$p(\lambda)=\lambda^{3}-2\lambda=2^{3}-2(2)=8-4=4$$

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A matrix $A$ of size $4\times 6$ has rank $3$. The dimension of its null space is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rank–nullity theorem: $\text{rank}(A)+\text{nullity}(A)=n$, where $n$ is the number of columns.

**Working.**
$$\text{nullity}=n-\text{rank}=6-3=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Differentiate term-by-term.

**Working.**
$$f'(x)=3x^{2}-3,\qquad f'(2)=3(4)-3=9$$

**Answer.** $9$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Evaluate $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{1}\left(3x^{2}+2x\right)\,dx$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Power rule for integration.

**Working.**
$$\int_{0}^{1}(3x^{2}+2x)\,dx=\big[x^{3}+x^{2}\big]_{0}^{1}=(1+1)-0=2$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin 3x}{x}=$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\lim_{u\to0}\dfrac{\sin u}{u}=1$.

**Working.**
$$\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin 3x}{x}=\lim_{x\to 0}3\cdot\frac{\sin 3x}{3x}=3(1)=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The local maximum value of $f(x)=x^{3}-6x^{2}+9x$ is _____ .

figure.caption: Cubic y = x^3 - 6x^2 + 9x with a local maximum at x = 1 (value 4) and local minimum at x = 3.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='40' x2='30' y2='175' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='170' x2='215' y2='170' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='30,170 52,76 75,50 98,69 120,110 142,151 165,170 188,144 210,50' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='75' cy='50' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><circle cx='165' cy='170' r='3' fill='#10b981'/><text x='80' y='46' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>local max</text><text x='150' y='186' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>x = 3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical points where $f'(x)=0$; classify with $f''$.

**Working.**
$$f'(x)=3x^{2}-12x+9=3(x-1)(x-3)$$
Critical points $x=1,3$. Since $f''(x)=6x-12$, $f''(1)=-6<0\Rightarrow$ local maximum at $x=1$.
$$f(1)=1-6+9=4$$

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The area of the region bounded by $y=x$ and $y=x^{2}$ between $x=0$ and $x=1$ (shaded) is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Region enclosed between the line y = x and the parabola y = x² on [0, 1].
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='170' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='170' x2='205' y2='170' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M30 170 L170 30 L135 91.25 L100 135 L65 161.25 Z' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.18)' stroke='none'/><polyline points='30,170 170,30' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><polyline points='30,170 65,161.25 100,135 135,91.25 170,30' fill='none' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><text x='148' y='44' font-size='11' fill='#6366f1'>y = x</text><text x='150' y='150' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>y = x^2</text><text x='160' y='184' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>x = 1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area between two curves $=\int (\text{upper}-\text{lower})\,dx$. On $[0,1]$, $x\ge x^{2}$.

**Working.**
$$A=\int_{0}^{1}\left(x-x^{2}\right)dx=\left[\tfrac{x^{2}}{2}-\tfrac{x^{3}}{3}\right]_{0}^{1}=\tfrac12-\tfrac13=\tfrac16\approx 0.17$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac16\approx 0.17$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: In the Maclaurin (Taylor about $0$) series of $e^{x}$, the coefficient of $x^{3}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $e^{x}=\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}\dfrac{x^{n}}{n!}$.

**Working.** Coefficient of $x^{3}$ is $\dfrac{1}{3!}=\dfrac{1}{6}$.

**Answer.** $\tfrac16$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $f(x,y)=x^{3}y^{2}$, the mixed partial derivative $\dfrac{\partial^{2}f}{\partial x\,\partial y}$ at $(1,1)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Differentiate successively; order does not matter for this smooth function.

**Working.**
$$\frac{\partial f}{\partial y}=2x^{3}y,\qquad \frac{\partial}{\partial x}\left(2x^{3}y\right)=6x^{2}y$$
At $(1,1)$: $6(1)(1)=6$.

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{1-\cos x}{x^{2}}=$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Use $1-\cos x\approx \tfrac{x^{2}}{2}$ for small $x$ (or L'Hôpital twice).

**Working.**
$$\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{1-\cos x}{x^{2}}=\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{2x}=\frac12$$

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following infinite series CONVERGES?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The $p$-series $\sum n^{-p}$ converges iff $p>1$.

**Working.** Here $p=1$ (harmonic, diverges), $p=2$ (converges), $p=\tfrac12$ (diverges), and $\sum 1$ clearly diverges.

**Answer.** $\sum 1/n^{2}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Evaluate $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{2} x\,e^{x^{2}}\,dx$ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Substitution $u=x^{2}$, $du=2x\,dx$.

**Working.**
$$\int_{0}^{2}x e^{x^{2}}dx=\tfrac12\int_{0}^{4}e^{u}\,du=\tfrac12\left(e^{4}-1\right)=\tfrac12(54.598-1)\approx 26.80$$

**Answer.** $\approx 26.80$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which statements about $f(x)=|x|$ are correct?
options: It has a global minimum at $x=0$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continuity vs differentiability of the absolute-value function.

**Working.**
- Continuous at $0$ — TRUE.
- Differentiable at $0$ — FALSE (left slope $-1$, right slope $+1$).
- Global minimum at $0$ (value $0$) — TRUE.
- $f'(0)$ does not exist, so not $0$ — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a) and (c).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $x>0$, the minimum value of $f(x)=x+\dfrac{1}{x}$ is _____ .

figure.caption: Curve y = x + 1/x for x > 0, attaining its minimum value 2 at x = 1.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='40' x2='30' y2='175' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='215' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='50,67 60,125 72,151 90,160 120,148 150,125 180,97 210,67' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='90' cy='160' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='95' y='156' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>min = 2 at x = 1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Set $f'(x)=0$ and verify a minimum (or use AM–GM).

**Working.**
$$f'(x)=1-\tfrac{1}{x^{2}}=0\Rightarrow x=1,\qquad f''(x)=\tfrac{2}{x^{3}}>0$$
So a minimum at $x=1$: $f(1)=1+1=2$. (AM–GM: $x+\tfrac1x\ge 2$.)

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The eigenvalues of the diagonal matrix $A=\operatorname{diag}(2,\,-3,\,5)$ are
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a diagonal (or triangular) matrix the eigenvalues are exactly the diagonal entries.

**Working.** The characteristic polynomial factorises as $(2-\lambda)(-3-\lambda)(5-\lambda)=0$.

**Answer.** $2,\,-3,\,5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The eigenvalues of an invertible matrix $A$ are $2$ and $5$. The smallest eigenvalue of $A^{-1}$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** If $\lambda$ is an eigenvalue of $A$, then $1/\lambda$ is an eigenvalue of $A^{-1}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{eigenvalues of }A^{-1}=\tfrac12,\;\tfrac15$$
The smallest is $\tfrac15=0.2$.

**Answer.** $0.2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For an orthogonal matrix $Q$ (i.e. $Q^{T}Q=I$), the determinant $\det(Q)$ can be
questions.solution:
**Concept.** From $Q^{T}Q=I$, $\det(Q)^{2}=\det(Q^{T})\det(Q)=\det(I)=1$.

**Working.**
$$\det(Q)^{2}=1\;\Rightarrow\;\det(Q)=\pm 1$$

**Answer.** $+1$ or $-1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A $3\times3$ matrix $A$ has eigenvalues $1,\,2,\,3$. The trace of $A^{2}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eigenvalues of $A^{2}$ are $\lambda_i^{2}$, and the trace equals the sum of eigenvalues.

**Working.**
$$\operatorname{tr}(A^{2})=1^{2}+2^{2}+3^{2}=1+4+9=14$$

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A $2\times2$ matrix with a repeated eigenvalue $\lambda$ is diagonalizable if and only if
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A matrix is diagonalizable iff the geometric multiplicity equals the algebraic multiplicity for every eigenvalue.

**Working.** With a single repeated eigenvalue $\lambda$ (algebraic multiplicity $2$), diagonalizability needs two independent eigenvectors, i.e. $A-\lambda I=0$, so $A=\lambda I$. (Any $2\times2$ matrix has $\det=\lambda^{2}$ for a repeated eigenvalue, so that condition is automatic and insufficient.)

**Answer.** $A=\lambda I$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The determinant of $\begin{bmatrix}3 & 4\\ 1 & 2\end{bmatrix}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\det\begin{bmatrix}a & b\\ c & d\end{bmatrix}=ad-bc$.

**Working.**
$$\det=(3)(2)-(4)(1)=6-4=2$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following is ALWAYS shared by a matrix $A$ and its transpose $A^{T}$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\det(A^{T}-\lambda I)=\det\big((A-\lambda I)^{T}\big)=\det(A-\lambda I)$, so they share the characteristic polynomial.

**Working.** Equal characteristic polynomials $\Rightarrow$ identical eigenvalues, but the eigenvectors generally differ.

**Answer.** Eigenvalues.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the system $2x+3y=8,\; x+y=3$, the value of $y$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Solve by elimination/substitution.

**Working.** From $x=3-y$: $2(3-y)+3y=8\Rightarrow 6+y=8\Rightarrow y=2$ (and $x=1$).

**Answer.** $y=2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A square matrix is singular if and only if
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A matrix is invertible iff $\det\neq0$; singular means non-invertible.

**Working.** $\det(A)=0\iff A$ has a zero eigenvalue $\iff$ columns are linearly dependent.

**Answer.** Its determinant is zero.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $A=\begin{bmatrix}1 & 2\\ 3 & 4\end{bmatrix}$, the $(1,1)$ entry of $A^{-1}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $A^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{\det A}\begin{bmatrix}d & -b\\ -c & a\end{bmatrix}$ for $A=\begin{bmatrix}a & b\\ c & d\end{bmatrix}$.

**Working.** $\det A=4-6=-2$, so
$$A^{-1}=\frac{1}{-2}\begin{bmatrix}4 & -2\\ -3 & 1\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}-2 & 1\\ 1.5 & -0.5\end{bmatrix}$$
The $(1,1)$ entry is $-2$.

**Answer.** $-2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which elementary row operations leave the determinant of a square matrix UNCHANGED?
options: Adding a multiple of one row to another
Multiplying a row by a non-zero scalar $k$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Effect of elementary operations on the determinant.

**Working.**
- Add a multiple of one row to another — determinant unchanged (TRUE).
- Swap two rows — sign flips (FALSE).
- Multiply a row by $k$ — determinant scales by $k$ (FALSE).
- Transpose — $\det(A^{T})=\det(A)$ (TRUE).

**Answer.** Options (a) and (d).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The trace of $\begin{bmatrix}5 & 2 & 1\\ 0 & 3 & 4\\ 7 & 1 & 2\end{bmatrix}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Trace = sum of the leading-diagonal entries.

**Working.**
$$\operatorname{tr}=5+3+2=10$$

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The rank of $\begin{bmatrix}1 & 2 & 1\\ 2 & 4 & 2\\ 3 & 6 & 3\end{bmatrix}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rank = number of linearly independent rows.

**Working.** Here $R_2=2R_1$ and $R_3=3R_1$, so only one independent row remains.
$$\text{rank}=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $\lambda=4$ is an eigenvalue of $A$, the corresponding eigenvalue of $3A+2I$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** If $Av=\lambda v$, then $(3A+2I)v=(3\lambda+2)v$.

**Working.**
$$3\lambda+2=3(4)+2=14$$

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A homogeneous linear system $Ax=0$ with more unknowns than equations has
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A homogeneous system is always consistent ($x=0$ works); with more unknowns than equations there are free variables.

**Working.** Free variables $\Rightarrow$ non-trivial solutions exist, hence infinitely many.

**Answer.** Infinitely many solutions.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The system $x+y+z=6,\; x+2y+3z=14,\; x+3y+kz=20$ fails to have a unique solution when $k=$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Unique solution requires a non-zero coefficient determinant.

**Working.**
$$\det\begin{bmatrix}1 & 1 & 1\\ 1 & 2 & 3\\ 1 & 3 & k\end{bmatrix}=1(2k-9)-1(k-3)+1(3-2)=k-5$$
Setting $k-5=0$ gives $k=5$.

**Answer.** $k=5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Two similar matrices ($B=P^{-1}AP$) share all of the following EXCEPT
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Similarity preserves the characteristic polynomial (hence determinant, trace, eigenvalues, rank) but not the eigenvectors.

**Working.** If $Av=\lambda v$ then $B(P^{-1}v)=\lambda(P^{-1}v)$, so eigenvectors are transformed by $P^{-1}$ and differ in general.

**Answer.** Eigenvectors.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a $3\times3$ matrix $A$ with $\det(A)=4$, the value of $\det(2A)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\det(kA)=k^{n}\det(A)$ for an $n\times n$ matrix.

**Working.**
$$\det(2A)=2^{3}\det(A)=8\times 4=32$$

**Answer.** $32$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For an invertible $n\times n$ matrix $A$, which statements are TRUE?
options: The columns of $A$ are linearly independent
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equivalent conditions for invertibility.

**Working.**
- $\det(A)\neq0$ — TRUE.
- Columns linearly independent — TRUE.
- $0$ an eigenvalue — FALSE (would force $\det=0$).
- Full rank $n$ — TRUE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (d).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The linear transformation represented by $A=\begin{bmatrix}0 & -1\\ 1 & 0\end{bmatrix}$ maps the vector $(1,0)$ as shown. It is a

figure.caption: The unit vector (1,0) is mapped to (0,1): a 90 degree anticlockwise rotation.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='160' x2='200' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='110' y1='185' x2='110' y2='20' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><defs><marker id='ar' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#6366f1'/></marker></defs><line x1='110' y1='160' x2='180' y2='160' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2.5' marker-end='url(#ar)'/><line x1='110' y1='160' x2='110' y2='90' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2.5' marker-end='url(#ar)'/><path d='M150 160 A40 40 0 0 0 110 120' fill='none' stroke='#f59e0b' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='160' y='176' font-size='11' fill='#6366f1'>(1,0)</text><text x='116' y='96' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>(0,1)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply $A$ to the basis vectors and identify the standard transformation.

**Working.**
$$A\begin{bmatrix}1\\0\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}0\\1\end{bmatrix},\qquad A\begin{bmatrix}0\\1\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}-1\\0\end{bmatrix}$$
This is exactly the rotation matrix $\begin{bmatrix}\cos90^{\circ} & -\sin90^{\circ}\\ \sin90^{\circ} & \cos90^{\circ}\end{bmatrix}$.

**Answer.** Anticlockwise rotation by $90^{\circ}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $f(x)=\ln x$, then $f'(2)=$ _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\dfrac{d}{dx}\ln x=\dfrac{1}{x}$.

**Working.**
$$f'(2)=\tfrac{1}{2}=0.5$$

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 2}\frac{x^{2}-4}{x-2}=$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Factor and cancel the removable singularity.

**Working.**
$$\frac{x^{2}-4}{x-2}=\frac{(x-2)(x+2)}{x-2}=x+2\to 4$$

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{e^{x}-1-x}{x^{2}}=$ _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Expand $e^{x}=1+x+\tfrac{x^{2}}{2}+\cdots$ (or apply L'Hôpital twice).

**Working.**
$$\frac{e^{x}-1-x}{x^{2}}=\frac{\tfrac{x^{2}}{2}+\tfrac{x^{3}}{6}+\cdots}{x^{2}}\to \frac12$$

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Rolle's theorem applies to $f$ on $[a,b]$ provided $f$ is continuous on $[a,b]$, differentiable on $(a,b)$, and

figure.caption: Rolle's theorem: f(a) = f(b) implies a point c in (a, b) with a horizontal tangent.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='150' x2='220' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M40 130 Q130 30 220 130' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='90' y1='55' x2='170' y2='55' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='130' cy='52' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><line x1='40' y1='150' x2='40' y2='130' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='220' y1='150' x2='220' y2='130' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='34' y='165' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>a</text><text x='216' y='165' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>b</text><text x='124' y='165' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>c</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Hypotheses of Rolle's theorem.

**Working.** The extra equal-endpoint condition $f(a)=f(b)$ guarantees a point $c\in(a,b)$ with $f'(c)=0$.

**Answer.** $f(a)=f(b)$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $f(x,y)=x^{2}y+y^{3}$, the partial derivative $\dfrac{\partial f}{\partial x}$ at $(2,1)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Treat $y$ as constant when differentiating with respect to $x$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{\partial f}{\partial x}=2xy,\qquad \text{at }(2,1):\;2(2)(1)=4$$

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: In the Maclaurin series of $\cos x$, the coefficient of $x^{2}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\cos x=1-\dfrac{x^{2}}{2!}+\dfrac{x^{4}}{4!}-\cdots$.

**Working.** Coefficient of $x^{2}$ is $-\dfrac{1}{2!}=-\dfrac{1}{2}$.

**Answer.** $-\tfrac12$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The minimum value of $f(x,y)=x^{2}+y^{2}-4x-6y+13$ over all real $(x,y)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Complete the square to write $f$ as a sum of squares plus a constant.

**Working.**
$$f=(x-2)^{2}+(y-3)^{2}+13-4-9=(x-2)^{2}+(y-3)^{2}$$
The minimum occurs at $(2,3)$ with value $0$.

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to\infty}\frac{3x^{2}+2}{x^{2}+5}=$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a rational function with equal-degree numerator and denominator, the limit at infinity is the ratio of leading coefficients.

**Working.**
$$\lim_{x\to\infty}\frac{3x^{2}+2}{x^{2}+5}=\frac{3}{1}=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Evaluate $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{1} x\,e^{x}\,dx$ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Integration by parts $\int u\,dv=uv-\int v\,du$ with $u=x$, $dv=e^{x}dx$.

**Working.**
$$\int_{0}^{1}x e^{x}dx=\big[x e^{x}-e^{x}\big]_{0}^{1}=(e-e)-(0-1)=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following functions are continuous for ALL real $x$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Identify points of discontinuity.

**Working.**
- $e^{x}$ — continuous everywhere (TRUE).
- $|x|$ — continuous everywhere (TRUE).
- $1/x$ — undefined at $x=0$ (FALSE).
- $x^{2}$ — polynomial, continuous everywhere (TRUE).

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (d).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Evaluate $\displaystyle\int_{1}^{e}\frac{1}{x}\,dx$ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\int \tfrac{1}{x}\,dx=\ln|x|$.

**Working.**
$$\int_{1}^{e}\frac{1}{x}dx=\ln e-\ln 1=1-0=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Fourier series of an ODD periodic function (such as the square wave shown) contains

figure.caption: An odd square wave: f(-x) = -f(x).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='10' y1='80' x2='250' y2='80' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='130' y1='20' x2='130' y2='140' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='10,120 70,120 70,40 130,40 130,120 190,120 190,40 250,40' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><text x='200' y='34' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>+A</text><text x='40' y='134' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>-A</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** An odd function $f(-x)=-f(x)$ has all cosine (even) Fourier coefficients zero, including the constant $a_0$.

**Working.** Only the sine terms $b_n\sin(n\omega x)$ survive.

**Answer.** Only sine terms.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Evaluate the double integral $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{1}\int_{0}^{x}(x+y)\,dy\,dx$ over the triangular region shown (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Triangular region 0 <= y <= x, 0 <= x <= 1.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='180' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M30 150 L160 150 L160 20 Z' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.18)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1.5'/><text x='150' y='100' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>y = x</text><text x='120' y='164' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>x = 1</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Iterate: integrate over $y$ first, then $x$.

**Working.**
$$\int_{0}^{x}(x+y)\,dy=\Big[xy+\tfrac{y^{2}}{2}\Big]_{0}^{x}=x^{2}+\tfrac{x^{2}}{2}=\tfrac{3x^{2}}{2}$$
$$\int_{0}^{1}\tfrac{3x^{2}}{2}\,dx=\tfrac{3}{2}\cdot\tfrac{1}{3}=\tfrac12=0.5$$

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: By the ratio test, the power series $\displaystyle\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}\frac{x^{n}}{n!}$ converges for
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Ratio test: $\lim\left|\dfrac{a_{n+1}}{a_n}\right|<1$ for convergence.

**Working.**
$$\left|\frac{x^{n+1}/(n+1)!}{x^{n}/n!}\right|=\frac{|x|}{n+1}\to 0<1\quad\forall x$$
The radius of convergence is infinite (this series is $e^{x}$).

**Answer.** All real $x$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The average value of $f(x)=x^{2}$ over the interval $[0,3]$ is _____ .

figure.caption: y = x^2 on [0, 3]; the horizontal line marks the average value (= 3) over the interval.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='40' x2='30' y2='175' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='170' x2='215' y2='170' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='30,170 90,157 150,116 210,49' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='210' y2='130' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='5 3'/><text x='150' y='124' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>average = 3</text><text x='198' y='184' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>x = 3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average value $=\dfrac{1}{b-a}\int_a^b f\,dx$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{3-0}\int_{0}^{3}x^{2}\,dx=\frac{1}{3}\cdot\frac{27}{3}=\frac{1}{3}\cdot 9=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The slope of the tangent to $y=x^{2}$ at $x=3$ is _____ .

figure.caption: Parabola y = x^2 with the tangent line at x = 3 (slope 6).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='40' x2='30' y2='175' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='170' x2='215' y2='170' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='30,170 90,157 150,116 210,49' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='120' y1='152' x2='210' y2='49' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5'/><circle cx='180' cy='86' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='150' y='80' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>slope = 6</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Slope $=\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ evaluated at the point.

**Working.**
$$\frac{dy}{dx}=2x,\qquad \text{at }x=3:\;2(3)=6$$

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The point of inflection of $f(x)=x^{3}-3x^{2}$ occurs at $x=$

figure.caption: Cubic y = x^3 - 3x^2 with a point of inflection at x = 1 (concavity changes sign).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='75' y1='30' x2='75' y2='175' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='60' x2='215' y2='60' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><polyline points='30,160 75,60 120,110 165,160 210,60' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='120' cy='110' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='126' y='106' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>inflection (x = 1)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Inflection where $f''(x)=0$ and concavity changes.

**Working.**
$$f''(x)=6x-6=0\;\Rightarrow\;x=1$$
Concavity switches sign there, confirming inflection.

**Answer.** $x=1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\tan x-x}{x^{3}}=$ _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Use $\tan x=x+\dfrac{x^{3}}{3}+\cdots$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{\tan x-x}{x^{3}}=\frac{\tfrac{x^{3}}{3}+\cdots}{x^{3}}\to \frac13\approx 0.33$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac13\approx 0.33$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: $\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^{n}=$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Geometric series $\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}ar^{n-1}=\dfrac{a}{1-r}$ for $|r|<1$; here first term $a=\tfrac12$, ratio $r=\tfrac12$.

**Working.**
$$S=\frac{1/2}{1-1/2}=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $f(x)=\sin x$, the value of the fifth derivative $f^{(5)}(0)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maclaurin series $\sin x=x-\dfrac{x^{3}}{3!}+\dfrac{x^{5}}{5!}-\cdots$; coefficient of $x^{5}$ is $\dfrac{f^{(5)}(0)}{5!}$.

**Working.** The coefficient of $x^{5}$ is $\dfrac{1}{5!}$, so $f^{(5)}(0)=5!\cdot\dfrac{1}{5!}=1$.

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The alternating series $\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}\frac{(-1)^{n+1}}{n}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** It converges by the alternating-series (Leibniz) test, but $\sum 1/n$ diverges.

**Working.** Terms decrease to $0$ in magnitude, so the alternating series converges (to $\ln 2$); the absolute series is the harmonic series, which diverges. Hence convergence is conditional.

**Answer.** Conditionally convergent.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $z=x^{2}y$, the estimated change $dz$ at $(2,3)$ when $dx=0.01$ and $dy=0.02$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total differential $dz=\dfrac{\partial z}{\partial x}dx+\dfrac{\partial z}{\partial y}dy$.

**Working.**
$$dz=2xy\,dx+x^{2}\,dy=2(2)(3)(0.01)+(4)(0.02)=0.12+0.08=0.20$$

**Answer.** $0.20$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which properties of definite integrals are ALWAYS valid?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Basic integral properties.

**Working.**
- Reversing limits flips sign — TRUE.
- Zero-width interval gives $0$ — TRUE.
- Linearity (sum rule) — TRUE.
- Integral of a product is NOT the product of integrals — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (c).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Evaluate the improper integral $\displaystyle\int_{1}^{\infty}\frac{1}{x^{2}}\,dx$ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\int_1^\infty x^{-2}dx=\lim_{b\to\infty}\big[-x^{-1}\big]_1^{b}$.

**Working.**
$$\lim_{b\to\infty}\left(-\frac{1}{b}+1\right)=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: By the Mean Value Theorem, the value $c\in(1,3)$ satisfying $f'(c)=\dfrac{f(3)-f(1)}{3-1}$ for $f(x)=x^{2}$ is

figure.caption: Mean Value Theorem: the tangent at c is parallel to the secant joining the endpoints.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='150' x2='220' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M40 135 Q130 20 215 70' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='40' y1='135' x2='215' y2='70' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1.5'/><line x1='95' y1='90' x2='185' y2='57' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='140' cy='73' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='110' y='40' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>secant</text><text x='120' y='100' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>tangent at c</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** MVT: there exists $c$ with $f'(c)$ equal to the average rate of change.

**Working.**
$$\frac{f(3)-f(1)}{2}=\frac{9-1}{2}=4,\qquad f'(c)=2c=4\Rightarrow c=2$$

**Answer.** $c=2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The area enclosed between the parabola $y=4-x^{2}$ and the $x$-axis (shaded) is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Region bounded by y = 4 - x^2 and the x-axis, from x = -2 to x = 2.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='140' x2='220' y2='140' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='120' y1='20' x2='120' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M40 140 Q120 -12 200 140 Z' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.18)' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2'/><text x='150' y='60' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>y = 4 - x^2</text><text x='34' y='154' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>-2</text><text x='196' y='154' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The curve crosses the axis at $x=\pm2$; area $=\int_{-2}^{2}(4-x^{2})\,dx$.

**Working.**
$$\int_{-2}^{2}(4-x^{2})\,dx=\Big[4x-\tfrac{x^{3}}{3}\Big]_{-2}^{2}=2\left(8-\tfrac{8}{3}\right)=\tfrac{32}{3}\approx 10.67$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac{32}{3}\approx 10.67$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $f(x,y,z)=x^{2}+y^{2}+z^{2}$, the value of $\dfrac{\partial f}{\partial z}$ at $(1,2,3)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Partial derivative w.r.t. $z$ treats $x,y$ as constants.

**Working.**
$$\frac{\partial f}{\partial z}=2z,\qquad \text{at }z=3:\;2(3)=6$$

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The divergence of $\mathbf{F}=x\,\hat{i}+y\,\hat{j}+z\,\hat{k}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=\dfrac{\partial F_x}{\partial x}+\dfrac{\partial F_y}{\partial y}+\dfrac{\partial F_z}{\partial z}$.

**Working.**
$$\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=1+1+1=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $f=x^{2}+yz$, the directional derivative at $(1,1,1)$ in the direction of $\mathbf{a}=3\hat{j}+4\hat{k}$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: On a simply-connected domain, a vector field $\mathbf{F}$ is conservative if and only if
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A field is conservative iff it is irrotational (curl-free) on a simply-connected region, i.e. $\mathbf{F}=
abla\phi$.

**Working.** $
abla	imes(
abla\phi)=\mathbf{0}$, so a potential exists precisely when $
abla	imes\mathbf{F}=\mathbf{0}$.

**Answer.** $
abla	imes\mathbf{F}=\mathbf{0}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The curl of $\mathbf{F}=-y\,\hat{i}+x\,\hat{j}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\nabla\times\mathbf{F}=\left(\partial_y F_z-\partial_z F_y,\ \partial_z F_x-\partial_x F_z,\ \partial_x F_y-\partial_y F_x\right)$.

**Working.** With $F_x=-y,\,F_y=x,\,F_z=0$:
$$\nabla\times\mathbf{F}=(0,\,0,\,1-(-1))=2\,\hat{k}$$

**Answer.** $2\,\hat{k}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For any smooth vector field $\mathbf{F}$, the value of $\nabla\cdot(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The divergence of a curl is identically zero.

**Working.**
$$\nabla\cdot(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})=0$$

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the conservative field $\mathbf{F}=\nabla\phi$ with $\phi=xy$, the line integral $\int_{C}\mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r}$ along the path $C$ from $(0,0)$ to $(2,3)$ is _____ .

figure.caption: A path C from (0,0) to (2,3); the integral of a gradient field is path-independent.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='145' x2='185' y2='145' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='20' x2='25' y2='145' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M25 145 Q90 60 150 50' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='25' cy='145' r='3' fill='#10b981'/><circle cx='150' cy='50' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='30' y='160' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>(0,0)</text><text x='130' y='42' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>(2,3)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a gradient field, $\int_C\nabla\phi\cdot d\mathbf{r}=\phi(\text{end})-\phi(\text{start})$ (path-independent).

**Working.**
$$\phi(2,3)-\phi(0,0)=(2)(3)-0=6$$

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Green's theorem relates a line integral around a closed plane curve $C$ to
options: a double integral over the enclosed region
a surface integral over a sphere
a triple integral over a volume
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\oint_C(P\,dx+Q\,dy)=\iint_R\left(\dfrac{\partial Q}{\partial x}-\dfrac{\partial P}{\partial y}\right)dA$.

**Working.** The right-hand side is a double (area) integral over the region $R$ bounded by $C$.

**Answer.** A double integral over the enclosed region.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Using Green's theorem, the value of $\displaystyle\oint_{C}(x\,dy-y\,dx)$ where $C$ is the circle of radius $2$ (counter-clockwise) is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Counter-clockwise circle of radius 2; the line integral equals twice the enclosed area.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='15' y1='90' x2='165' y2='90' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='90' y1='15' x2='90' y2='165' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><circle cx='90' cy='90' r='55' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.15)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M145 84 a55 55 0 0 0 -6 -16' fill='none' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='139,68 134,74 142,77' fill='#ef4444'/><text x='118' y='66' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>r = 2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\oint_C(x\,dy-y\,dx)=\iint_R 2\,dA=2\,(\text{Area})$.

**Working.**
$$2\cdot\pi r^{2}=2\pi(2)^{2}=8\pi\approx 25.13$$

**Answer.** $8\pi\approx 25.13$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Gauss divergence theorem converts a closed surface (flux) integral into a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\oiint_{S}\mathbf{F}\cdot\hat{n}\,dS=\iiint_{V}(\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F})\,dV$.

**Working.** The flux through a closed surface equals the volume integral of the divergence over the enclosed region.

**Answer.** Volume integral of the divergence.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The outward flux of $\mathbf{F}=x\,\hat{i}+y\,\hat{j}+z\,\hat{k}$ through the surface of the unit cube $[0,1]^{3}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** By the divergence theorem, flux $=\iiint_V(\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F})\,dV$.

**Working.** $\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=3$ and the cube has volume $1$:
$$\text{flux}=3\times 1=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following vector fields are solenoidal (divergence-free)?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Solenoidal means $\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=0$.

**Working.**
- $(y,-x)$: $0+0=0$ — TRUE.
- $(x,y)$: $1+1=2\neq0$ — FALSE.
- $(z,x,y)$: $0+0+0=0$ — TRUE.
- $(2x,-2y)$: $2-2=0$ — TRUE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (c) and (d).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The divergence of $\mathbf{F}=2x\,\hat{i}+3y\,\hat{j}-5z\,\hat{k}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum the diagonal partials.

**Working.**
$$\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=2+3-5=0$$

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Stokes' theorem equates the surface integral of $(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})$ over $S$ to
options: the line integral of $\mathbf{F}$ around the boundary of $S$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\iint_S(\nabla\times\mathbf{F})\cdot\hat{n}\,dS=\oint_{\partial S}\mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r}$.

**Working.** The curl-flux through a surface equals the circulation of $\mathbf{F}$ around its boundary curve.

**Answer.** The line integral of $\mathbf{F}$ around the boundary of $S$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The magnitude of $\nabla f$ for $f(x,y)=3x+4y$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\nabla f=(f_x,f_y)$; take its magnitude.

**Working.**
$$\nabla f=(3,4),\qquad |\nabla f|=\sqrt{3^{2}+4^{2}}=5$$

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: At any point, the gradient vector $\nabla f$ points in the direction of
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\nabla f$ is normal to level curves and points toward steepest ascent.

**Working.** The directional derivative is maximised along $\nabla f$, with value $|\nabla f|$.

**Answer.** Maximum rate of increase of $f$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Using $\iiint_V dV$, the volume of a sphere of radius $2$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Volume of a sphere $=\tfrac{4}{3}\pi r^{3}$ (the triple integral of $1$ over the ball).

**Working.**
$$V=\tfrac{4}{3}\pi(2)^{3}=\tfrac{32}{3}\pi\approx 33.51$$

**Answer.** $\approx 33.51$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Laplacian $\nabla^{2}f$ of $f=x^{2}+y^{2}+z^{2}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\nabla^{2}f=f_{xx}+f_{yy}+f_{zz}$.

**Working.**
$$f_{xx}=f_{yy}=f_{zz}=2\;\Rightarrow\;\nabla^{2}f=6$$

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The work done by the constant force $\mathbf{F}=3\,\hat{i}+4\,\hat{j}$ in moving a particle along the straight line from $(0,0)$ to $(2,1)$ is _____ .

figure.caption: Constant force F = (3,4) acting over displacement from (0,0) to (2,1).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='120' x2='185' y2='120' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='20' x2='25' y2='120' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><defs><marker id='af' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#6366f1'/></marker></defs><line x1='25' y1='120' x2='145' y2='80' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='2.5' marker-end='url(#af)'/><line x1='25' y1='120' x2='95' y2='30' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='3 2' marker-end='url(#af)'/><text x='120' y='95' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>d=(2,1)</text><text x='80' y='28' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>F=(3,4)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a constant force, $W=\mathbf{F}\cdot\mathbf{d}$.

**Working.**
$$W=(3)(2)+(4)(1)=6+4=10$$

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A vector field is termed irrotational when
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Irrotational $\equiv$ curl-free.

**Working.** $\nabla\times\mathbf{F}=\mathbf{0}$ defines an irrotational field (locally a gradient).

**Answer.** $\nabla\times\mathbf{F}=\mathbf{0}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The maximum value of the directional derivative of $f=xyz$ at the point $(1,2,3)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum directional derivative equals $|\nabla f|$.

**Working.** $\nabla f=(yz,\,xz,\,xy)=(6,3,2)$ at $(1,2,3)$.
$$|\nabla f|=\sqrt{36+9+4}=\sqrt{49}=7$$

**Answer.** $7$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a conservative vector field on a simply-connected domain, which statements are TRUE?
options: The circulation around any closed loop is non-zero
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equivalent characterisations of a conservative field.

**Working.**
- Path-independence — TRUE.
- Curl-free — TRUE.
- Existence of a potential — TRUE.
- Closed-loop circulation is zero, not non-zero — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (c).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The magnitude of the curl of the position vector $\mathbf{r}=x\,\hat{i}+y\,\hat{j}+z\,\hat{k}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Compute $\nabla\times\mathbf{r}$.

**Working.** Each component such as $\partial_y z-\partial_z y=0$, so $\nabla\times\mathbf{r}=\mathbf{0}$, magnitude $0$.

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For any twice-differentiable scalar field $f$, the quantity $\nabla\times(\nabla f)$ equals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The curl of a gradient is identically zero.

**Working.**
$$\nabla\times(\nabla f)=\mathbf{0}$$
This is why every gradient field is irrotational.

**Answer.** $\mathbf{0}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The value of $\displaystyle\oint_{C}(2x\,dx+2y\,dy)$ around any closed curve $C$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The integrand is the exact differential $d(x^{2}+y^{2})$, so the field $(2x,2y)=\nabla(x^{2}+y^{2})$ is conservative.

**Working.** The line integral of a gradient around any closed loop is $0$.

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=0$ everywhere, the net outward flux of $\mathbf{F}$ through ANY closed surface is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** By the divergence theorem, flux $=\iiint_V(\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F})\,dV$.

**Working.** A divergence-free field gives $\iiint_V 0\,dV=0$ for every closed surface.

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The directional derivative of $f=x^{2}+y^{2}$ at $(3,4)$ in the direction pointing toward the origin is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $D_{\hat u}f=\nabla f\cdot\hat u$, with $\hat u$ the unit vector toward the origin.

**Working.** $\nabla f=(2x,2y)=(6,8)$; direction to origin $=(-3,-4)/5=(-0.6,-0.8)$.
$$D_{\hat u}f=(6)(-0.6)+(8)(-0.8)=-3.6-6.4=-10$$

**Answer.** $-10$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The plane vector field sketched (arrows circulating about the origin) is best described as

figure.caption: Vector field F = (-y, x): arrows circulate anticlockwise about the origin.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='15' y1='100' x2='185' y2='100' stroke='#e2e8f0' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='100' y1='15' x2='100' y2='185' stroke='#e2e8f0' stroke-width='1'/><defs><marker id='av' markerWidth='7' markerHeight='7' refX='5' refY='2.5' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L5 2.5 L0 5 Z' fill='#6366f1'/></marker></defs><line x1='140' y1='60' x2='150' y2='90' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1.6' marker-end='url(#av)'/><line x1='140' y1='140' x2='110' y2='150' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1.6' marker-end='url(#av)'/><line x1='60' y1='140' x2='50' y2='110' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1.6' marker-end='url(#av)'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='90' y2='50' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='1.6' marker-end='url(#av)'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The circulating field is $\mathbf{F}=(-y,\,x)$.

**Working.** $\nabla\times\mathbf{F}=2\hat{k}\neq\mathbf{0}$, so the field is rotational (it has non-zero curl), even though $\nabla\cdot\mathbf{F}=0$.

**Answer.** Having non-zero curl (rotational).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The order of the differential equation $y'''+y=0$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order = highest derivative appearing.

**Working.** The highest derivative is $y'''$, so the order is $3$.

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the equation $(y'')^{2}+y=0$, the (order, degree) is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order = highest derivative; degree = power of the highest derivative once the equation is polynomial in derivatives.

**Working.** Highest derivative is $y''$ (order $2$), raised to power $2$ (degree $2$).

**Answer.** $(2,2)$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The solution of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}=x$ with $y(0)=1$ gives $y(2)=$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Integrate directly and apply the initial condition.

**Working.**
$$y=\tfrac{x^{2}}{2}+C,\quad y(0)=1\Rightarrow C=1,\quad y(2)=\tfrac{4}{2}+1=3$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The integrating factor of the linear ODE $y'+2y=e^{x}$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For $y'+P(x)y=Q(x)$, $\text{IF}=e^{\int P\,dx}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{IF}=e^{\int 2\,dx}=e^{2x}$$

**Answer.** $e^{2x}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The roots of the characteristic equation of $y''-5y'+6y=0$ are
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Substitute $y=e^{rx}$ to get the auxiliary equation.

**Working.**
$$r^{2}-5r+6=0\;\Rightarrow\;(r-2)(r-3)=0\;\Rightarrow\;r=2,3$$

**Answer.** $2,\,3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: An equation of the form $\dfrac{dy}{dx}+P(x)\,y=Q(x)$ is a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Linearity in $y$ and $y'$ with first derivative only.

**Working.** This standard form is a first-order linear ODE, solved by an integrating factor.

**Answer.** Linear first-order ODE.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The undamped oscillator $y''+4y=0$ has angular frequency

figure.caption: Undamped oscillator y'' + 4y = 0: a pure sinusoid of angular frequency 2.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='245' y2='80' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='20' x2='25' y2='140' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M25 80 Q50 20 75 80 T125 80 T175 80 T225 80' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><text x='180' y='135' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>period = pi</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $y''+\omega^{2}y=0$ has roots $\pm i\omega$ and solution $\cos\omega t,\sin\omega t$.

**Working.** $\omega^{2}=4\Rightarrow\omega=2$.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The equation $\dfrac{dy}{dx}=x\,y^{2}$ is best classified as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Separable if it factors as $g(y)\,dy=f(x)\,dx$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{dy}{y^{2}}=x\,dx$$
so it is separable.

**Answer.** Separable.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Laplace transform $\mathcal{L}\{e^{2t}\}$ evaluated at $s=5$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\mathcal{L}\{e^{at}\}=\dfrac{1}{s-a}$ for $s>a$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{s-2}\Big|_{s=5}=\frac{1}{3}\approx 0.33$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac13\approx 0.33$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Laplace transform of the constant function $f(t)=1$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\mathcal{L}\{1\}=\int_0^\infty e^{-st}\,dt=\dfrac{1}{s}$.

**Working.** Valid for $s>0$.

**Answer.** $\dfrac{1}{s}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $y''+y=0$ with $y(0)=1,\;y'(0)=0$, the value of $y\!\left(\tfrac{\pi}{3}\right)$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Solution is $y=A\cos t+B\sin t$; apply initial conditions.

**Working.** $y(0)=1\Rightarrow A=1$; $y'(0)=0\Rightarrow B=0$, so $y=\cos t$.
$$y\!\left(\tfrac{\pi}{3}\right)=\cos 60^{\circ}=0.5$$

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The general solution of $y''-y=0$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Characteristic roots $r^{2}-1=0\Rightarrow r=\pm1$ (real, distinct).

**Working.** Distinct real roots give exponential solutions $C_1 e^{t}+C_2 e^{-t}$.

**Answer.** $C_1 e^{t}+C_2 e^{-t}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The solution of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}+y=0$ with $y(0)=4$ gives $y(1)=$ _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Exponential decay y = 4 e^(-x), starting at y(0) = 4.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='135' x2='205' y2='135' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='15' x2='25' y2='135' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M25 25 C70 70 120 110 200 128' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='25' cy='25' r='3' fill='#10b981'/><text x='30' y='24' font-size='10' fill='#10b981'>y(0)=4</text><text x='120' y='90' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>y = 4 e^(-x)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Separable/linear; solution $y=y(0)e^{-x}$.

**Working.**
$$y=4e^{-x},\qquad y(1)=\frac{4}{e}\approx 1.47$$

**Answer.** $\approx 1.47$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The general solution of the Cauchy–Euler equation $x^{2}y''-3xy'+3y=0$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Try $y=x^{m}$; this yields an indicial equation.

**Working.**
$$m(m-1)-3m+3=m^{2}-4m+3=(m-1)(m-3)=0\Rightarrow m=1,3$$
Solution $C_1 x+C_2 x^{3}$.

**Answer.** $C_1 x+C_2 x^{3}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Laplace transform $\mathcal{L}\{t\}$ evaluated at $s=2$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following differential equations are LINEAR?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Linear means $y$ and its derivatives appear to the first power and are not multiplied together.

**Working.**
- $y'+x^{2}y=\sin x$ — linear (TRUE).
- $y''+y=0$ — linear (TRUE).
- $y'+y^{2}=x$ — $y^{2}$ term, non-linear (FALSE).
- $yy''=1$ — product $yy''$, non-linear (FALSE).

**Answer.** Options (a) and (b).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The order of $\dfrac{d^{2}y}{dx^{2}}+\left(\dfrac{dy}{dx}\right)^{3}=x$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order = highest derivative present (independent of the powers).

**Working.** Highest derivative is $y''$, so order $=2$ (the cube affects degree, not order).

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The general solution of $y''-4y'+4y=0$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Repeated root gives a solution with a factor of $x$.

**Working.**
$$r^{2}-4r+4=(r-2)^{2}=0\Rightarrow r=2\text{ (double)}$$
Solution $(C_1+C_2 x)e^{2x}$.

**Answer.** $(C_1+C_2 x)e^{2x}$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Laplace transform $\mathcal{L}\{\sin 2t\}$ evaluated at $s=2$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The partial differential equation $u_{xx}+u_{yy}=0$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Classify $Au_{xx}+Bu_{xy}+Cu_{yy}$ by the sign of $B^{2}-4AC$.

**Working.** Here $A=C=1,\,B=0$, so $B^{2}-4AC=-4<0$ — elliptic. This is Laplace's equation.

**Answer.** Elliptic (Laplace's equation).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The solution of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}=2x$ with $y(1)=2$ gives $y(3)=$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Integrate and apply the initial condition.

**Working.**
$$y=x^{2}+C,\quad y(1)=2\Rightarrow C=1,\quad y(3)=9+1=10$$

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The one-dimensional heat equation $u_t=\alpha\,u_{xx}$ is classified as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Diffusion equations with one time derivative and one second space derivative are parabolic.

**Working.** The discriminant condition gives parabolic type.

**Answer.** Parabolic.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The roots of the characteristic equation of $y''+2y'+5y=0$ are
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Use $r=\dfrac{-b\pm\sqrt{b^{2}-4ac}}{2a}$.

**Working.**
$$r=\frac{-2\pm\sqrt{4-20}}{2}=\frac{-2\pm 4i}{2}=-1\pm 2i$$

**Answer.** $-1\pm 2i$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The wave equation $u_{tt}=c^{2}u_{xx}$ is classified as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** With $A=c^{2},\,C=-1$ (after moving terms), $B^{2}-4AC>0$.

**Working.** A positive discriminant gives a hyperbolic PDE.

**Answer.** Hyperbolic.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: If $\mathcal{L}^{-1}\!\left\{\dfrac{2}{s^{2}+4}\right\}=f(t)$, then $f\!\left(\tfrac{\pi}{4}\right)=$ _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\mathcal{L}^{-1}\!\left\{\dfrac{a}{s^{2}+a^{2}}\right\}=\sin at$.

**Working.** $f(t)=\sin 2t$, so $f\!\left(\tfrac{\pi}{4}\right)=\sin\tfrac{\pi}{2}=1$.

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: To solve a separable equation $g(y)\,dy=f(x)\,dx$, one should
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Separation of variables.

**Working.** After separating, integrate each side independently: $\int g(y)\,dy=\int f(x)\,dx+C$.

**Answer.** Integrate both sides.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Radioactive decay follows $N=N_0 e^{-\lambda t}$. If the half-life is $10$ years, the decay constant $\lambda$ (per year) is _____ (round to 4 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Half-life $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{\lambda}$.

**Working.**
$$\lambda=\frac{\ln 2}{10}=\frac{0.6931}{10}=0.0693\ \text{yr}^{-1}$$

**Answer.** $0.0693$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The left side of $x\,y'+y=\cos x$ can be written compactly as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Product rule recognition.

**Working.**
$$\frac{d}{dx}(xy)=x\,y'+y$$
so the equation becomes $\dfrac{d}{dx}(xy)=\cos x$, integrable directly.

**Answer.** $\dfrac{d}{dx}(xy)$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The roots of the characteristic equation of $y''-y'-2y=0$ are
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Auxiliary equation from $y=e^{rx}$.

**Working.**
$$r^{2}-r-2=(r-2)(r+1)=0\Rightarrow r=2,\,-1$$

**Answer.** $2,\,-1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which statements about the Laplace transform are TRUE?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fundamental Laplace properties.

**Working.**
- Linearity — TRUE.
- Derivative rule $sF(s)-f(0)$ — TRUE.
- First shift theorem $F(s-a)$ — TRUE.
- $\mathcal{L}\{1\}=1/s$, not $s$ — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (c).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $y'+y=e^{2x}$, the coefficient $A$ in the particular solution $y_p=A e^{2x}$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Method of undetermined coefficients: substitute $y_p=Ae^{2x}$.

**Working.**
$$2Ae^{2x}+Ae^{2x}=e^{2x}\Rightarrow 3A=1\Rightarrow A=\tfrac13\approx 0.33$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac13\approx 0.33$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The order of $\dfrac{d^{3}y}{dx^{3}}+x\left(\dfrac{dy}{dx}\right)^{4}=\sin x$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order is the highest derivative, regardless of any powers.

**Working.** The highest derivative is $y'''$, so the order is $3$ (the exponent $4$ would be the degree of the first-derivative term, not the order).

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the Cauchy–Euler equation $x^{2}y''+xy'-y=0$, the positive value of $m$ in the trial solution $y=x^{m}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Substitute $y=x^{m}$ to obtain the indicial equation.

**Working.**
$$m(m-1)+m-1=m^{2}-1=0\Rightarrow m=\pm1$$
The positive root is $m=1$.

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The solution curve sketched (monotonically decreasing toward zero, never negative) is consistent with the ODE

figure.caption: A solution that decays monotonically toward zero.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='125' x2='205' y2='125' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='15' x2='25' y2='125' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M25 30 C70 75 120 105 200 120' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><text x='120' y='70' font-size='10' fill='#6366f1'>decaying</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Exponential decay arises from $y'=-ky$ with $k>0$, giving $y=y_0 e^{-kt}$.

**Working.** $y'=+y$ grows; $y''+y=0$ oscillates; $y'=x$ is a parabola. Only $y'=-ky$ decays monotonically to zero.

**Answer.** $y'=-ky,\;k>0$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The arithmetic mean of the data $2,\,4,\,6,\,8,\,10$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mean $=\dfrac{\sum x_i}{n}$.

**Working.**
$$\bar{x}=\frac{2+4+6+8+10}{5}=\frac{30}{5}=6$$

**Answer.** $6$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The median of the data $3,\,5,\,7,\,9,\,11$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For an odd-sized ordered set, the median is the middle value.

**Working.** The middle of $3,5,7,9,11$ is $7$.

**Answer.** $7$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The mode of the data $2,\,3,\,3,\,5,\,7$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mode = most frequently occurring value.

**Working.** The value $3$ occurs twice, more than any other.

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The population variance of $2,\,4,\,6$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\sigma^{2}=\dfrac{\sum(x_i-\bar{x})^{2}}{n}$.

**Working.** $\bar{x}=4$; deviations $-2,0,2$; squared $4,0,4$ sum $8$.
$$\sigma^{2}=\frac{8}{3}\approx 2.67$$

**Answer.** $2.67$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a binomial distribution with parameters $n$ and $p$, the mean is

figure.caption: Binomial probability mass function (n = 5, p = 0.5); the distribution is symmetric about the mean np.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='40' y='150' width='22' height='10' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='70' y='110' width='22' height='50' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='100' y='60' width='22' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='130' y='60' width='22' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='160' y='110' width='22' height='50' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='190' y='150' width='22' height='10' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='44' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>0</text><text x='104' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2</text><text x='194' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>5</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Binomial mean.

**Working.** $E[X]=np$ (and variance $=npq$ with $q=1-p$).

**Answer.** $np$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A binomial random variable has $n=5,\;p=0.2$. Its mean is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mean $=np$.

**Working.**
$$\mu=5\times 0.2=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A binomial random variable has $n=5,\;p=0.2$. Its variance is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Variance $=npq$ with $q=1-p$.

**Working.**
$$\sigma^{2}=5(0.2)(0.8)=0.8$$

**Answer.** $0.8$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For any valid discrete probability distribution, the sum of all probabilities equals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Probabilities of all outcomes are mutually exclusive and exhaustive.

**Working.**
$$\sum_i p_i=1$$

**Answer.** $1$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a Poisson distribution with mean $\lambda=3$, the probability $P(X=0)$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Poisson probability mass function with mean lambda = 3 (right-skewed, peak near k = 2, 3).
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='240' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='38' y='136' width='20' height='24' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='66' y='87' width='20' height='73' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='94' y='50' width='20' height='110' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='122' y='50' width='20' height='110' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='150' y='77' width='20' height='83' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='178' y='110' width='20' height='50' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='206' y='135' width='20' height='25' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='44' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>0</text><text x='100' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2</text><text x='212' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>6</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P(X=k)=\dfrac{e^{-\lambda}\lambda^{k}}{k!}$.

**Working.**
$$P(X=0)=e^{-3}=0.0498\approx 0.05$$

**Answer.** $\approx 0.05$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a Poisson distribution, the variance is equal to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A defining property of the Poisson distribution.

**Working.** Both mean and variance equal $\lambda$.

**Answer.** The mean $\lambda$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A fair coin is tossed $3$ times. The probability of getting exactly $2$ heads is _____ (round to 3 decimals).

figure.caption: Distribution of the number of heads in 3 fair tosses: probabilities 1/8, 3/8, 3/8, 1/8.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='45' y='120' width='28' height='40' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='85' y='40' width='28' height='120' fill='#10b981'/><rect x='125' y='40' width='28' height='120' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='165' y='120' width='28' height='40' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='55' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>0</text><text x='95' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>1</text><text x='135' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>2</text><text x='175' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>3</text><text x='86' y='34' font-size='9' fill='#10b981'>3/8</text></svg>

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a perfectly symmetric normal distribution, the relationship between mean, median and mode is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Symmetry of the normal curve.

**Working.** All three measures of central tendency coincide at the centre.

**Answer.** Mean $=$ median $=$ mode.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the standard normal distribution shown, the probability $P(Z<0)$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Standard normal curve, symmetric about Z = 0.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='125' x2='220' y2='125' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M30 125 C70 125 90 30 120 30 C150 30 170 125 210 125' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M30 125 C70 125 90 30 120 30 L120 125 Z' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.18)'/><line x1='120' y1='30' x2='120' y2='125' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='112' y='140' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>0</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The standard normal curve is symmetric about $Z=0$.

**Working.** Half the total area lies to the left of the mean.
$$P(Z<0)=0.5$$

**Answer.** $0.5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: By the empirical (68–95–99.7) rule, the approximate fraction of a normal population within one standard deviation of the mean is

figure.caption: Empirical rule: about 68% of a normal distribution lies within one standard deviation of the mean.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='140' x2='245' y2='140' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M20 140 C70 140 90 30 132 30 C175 30 195 140 245 140 Z' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.10)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='95' y='40' width='74' height='100' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.18)' stroke='none'/><line x1='95' y1='30' x2='95' y2='140' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='169' y1='30' x2='169' y2='140' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='110' y='90' font-size='11' fill='#10b981'>68%</text><text x='84' y='155' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>mu-sigma</text><text x='150' y='155' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>mu+sigma</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Empirical rule for the normal distribution.

**Working.** About $68\%$ lies within $\mu\pm\sigma$, $95\%$ within $\mu\pm2\sigma$, $99.7\%$ within $\mu\pm3\sigma$.

**Answer.** $68\%$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the standard normal distribution, the probability $P(-1<Z<1)$ (shaded) is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Standard normal curve with the region between Z = -1 and Z = +1 shaded.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='125' x2='220' y2='125' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M30 125 C70 125 90 30 120 30 C150 30 170 125 210 125' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M90 125 C100 110 110 35 120 30 C130 35 140 110 150 125 Z' fill='rgba(16,185,129,0.22)'/><line x1='90' y1='125' x2='90' y2='95' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='150' y1='125' x2='150' y2='95' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='80' y='140' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>-1</text><text x='146' y='140' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>+1</text></svg>

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The expected value of a single roll of a fair six-sided die is

figure.caption: Fair six-sided die: a uniform distribution with each outcome having probability 1/6.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='150' x2='225' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='35' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='66' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='97' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='128' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='159' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='190' y='50' width='24' height='100' fill='#6366f1'/><text x='42' y='163' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>1</text><text x='197' y='163' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>6</text><text x='95' y='42' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>P = 1/6</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $E[X]=\sum x_i p_i$ with $p_i=\tfrac16$.

**Working.**
$$E[X]=\frac{1+2+3+4+5+6}{6}=\frac{21}{6}=3.5$$

**Answer.** $3.5$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Pearson correlation coefficient $r$ always lies in the range
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $r$ is a normalised covariance.

**Working.** By the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality, $-1\le r\le 1$.

**Answer.** $[-1,1]$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the bivariate data shown, $r=0.8$, $s_x=2$ and $s_y=4$. The slope of the regression line of $y$ on $x$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Scatter of points with a positively sloped least-squares regression line.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='25' y1='135' x2='185' y2='135' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='25' y1='15' x2='25' y2='135' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><line x1='30' y1='125' x2='180' y2='35' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='45' cy='118' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='70' cy='100' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='95' cy='95' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='120' cy='72' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='150' cy='55' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/><circle cx='170' cy='42' r='3' fill='#6366f1'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Regression slope $b_{yx}=r\,\dfrac{s_y}{s_x}$.

**Working.**
$$b_{yx}=0.8\times\frac{4}{2}=1.6$$

**Answer.** $1.6$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which statements about the correlation coefficient $r$ are TRUE?
options: $r$ depends on the units of measurement
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Properties of the Pearson correlation coefficient.

**Working.**
- Dimensionless (normalised) — TRUE.
- $r=0$ means no linear association (other relationships may exist) — TRUE.
- $|r|=1$ is perfect linear correlation — TRUE.
- It is invariant to units/linear scaling — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (c).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The range of the data $5,\,8,\,12,\,3,\,10$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Range $=\max-\min$.

**Working.**
$$12-3=9$$

**Answer.** $9$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a positively (right) skewed distribution, the usual order of the measures is

figure.caption: Right (positively) skewed distribution: mode < median < mean.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='140' x2='245' y2='140' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M20 140 C55 140 65 40 85 40 C120 40 150 130 245 138 Z' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.12)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='85' y1='40' x2='85' y2='140' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='108' y1='70' x2='108' y2='140' stroke='#f59e0b' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='132' y1='95' x2='132' y2='140' stroke='#10b981' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='66' y='155' font-size='9' fill='#ef4444'>mode</text><text x='98' y='166' font-size='9' fill='#f59e0b'>median</text><text x='128' y='155' font-size='9' fill='#10b981'>mean</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A long right tail pulls the mean above the median and mode.

**Working.** Hence mode $<$ median $<$ mean for right skew.

**Answer.** Mode $<$ median $<$ mean.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Given $P(A)=0.3$ and $P(B\,|\,A)=0.5$, the joint probability $P(A\cap B)$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiplication rule $P(A\cap B)=P(A)\,P(B\,|\,A)$.

**Working.**
$$P(A\cap B)=0.3\times 0.5=0.15$$

**Answer.** $0.15$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Two events $A$ and $B$ are independent if and only if
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Definition of statistical independence.

**Working.** Independence means the joint probability factorises: $P(A\cap B)=P(A)P(B)$.

**Answer.** $P(A\cap B)=P(A)\,P(B)$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The probability that two fair dice show a sum of $7$ is

figure.caption: The 36 equally likely outcomes of two dice; the highlighted anti-diagonal are the 6 outcomes summing to 7.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g fill='#cbd5e1'><circle cx='40' cy='30' r='6'/><circle cx='65' cy='30' r='6'/><circle cx='90' cy='30' r='6'/><circle cx='115' cy='30' r='6'/><circle cx='140' cy='30' r='6'/><circle cx='165' cy='30' r='6'/><circle cx='40' cy='55' r='6'/><circle cx='65' cy='55' r='6'/><circle cx='90' cy='55' r='6'/><circle cx='115' cy='55' r='6'/><circle cx='140' cy='55' r='6'/><circle cx='40' cy='80' r='6'/><circle cx='65' cy='80' r='6'/><circle cx='90' cy='80' r='6'/><circle cx='115' cy='80' r='6'/><circle cx='40' cy='105' r='6'/><circle cx='65' cy='105' r='6'/><circle cx='90' cy='105' r='6'/><circle cx='40' cy='130' r='6'/><circle cx='65' cy='130' r='6'/><circle cx='40' cy='155' r='6'/></g><g fill='#ef4444'><circle cx='165' cy='55' r='6'/><circle cx='140' cy='80' r='6'/><circle cx='115' cy='105' r='6'/><circle cx='90' cy='130' r='6'/><circle cx='65' cy='155' r='6'/><circle cx='40' cy='180' r='6'/></g><text x='70' y='184' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>sum = 7 (6 outcomes)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count favourable outcomes over $36$ equally-likely outcomes.

**Working.** Sums of $7$: $(1,6),(2,5),(3,4),(4,3),(5,2),(6,1)=6$ outcomes.
$$P=\frac{6}{36}=\frac{1}{6}$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac16$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Poisson distribution is a good approximation to the binomial distribution when
options: $n$ is large and $p$ is small (so $np$ is moderate)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Poisson limit of the binomial.

**Working.** As $n\to\infty,\;p\to0$ with $np=\lambda$ fixed, the binomial tends to a Poisson($\lambda$).

**Answer.** $n$ large and $p$ small.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The standard deviation of the data $4,\,4,\,4,\,4$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Standard deviation measures spread about the mean.

**Working.** All values equal the mean ($4$), so every deviation is zero.
$$\sigma=0$$

**Answer.** $0$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The variance of any data set is always
options: greater than or equal to zero
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Variance is an average of squared deviations.

**Working.** A sum of squares cannot be negative, so $\sigma^{2}\ge 0$ (zero only when all values are identical).

**Answer.** Greater than or equal to zero.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a normal distribution with $\mu=50$ and $\sigma=10$, the $z$-score of the value $x=70$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $z=\dfrac{x-\mu}{\sigma}$.

**Working.**
$$z=\frac{70-50}{10}=2$$

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following are measures of CENTRAL TENDENCY?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central tendency vs dispersion.

**Working.**
- Mean, median, mode locate the centre — TRUE.
- Standard deviation measures spread (dispersion) — FALSE.

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (c).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: A discrete random variable $X$ takes values $0,1,2$ with probabilities $0.2,\,0.5,\,0.3$. Its expected value $E[X]$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The distribution sketched has a long tail to the right. It is

figure.caption: A distribution with a long right tail.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='125' x2='225' y2='125' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M30 125 C50 120 60 40 85 40 C110 40 130 110 175 120 C200 124 210 125 215 125' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.18)' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><text x='150' y='100' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>long right tail</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Skew direction follows the longer tail.

**Working.** A tail extending to the right indicates positive (right) skew, with mean $>$ median $>$ mode.

**Answer.** Positively (right) skewed.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For the standard normal distribution, the probability $P(0<Z<2)$ (shaded) is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

figure.caption: Standard normal curve with the region between Z = 0 and Z = 2 shaded.
